episode.working_title: Self-host vs API: the break-even, re-measured

pricing_input._fill: VERIFIED 2026-06-12 from https://groq.com/pricing (llama-3.1-8b-instant 128k, 840 TPS listed). Re-glance before recording; rates were stable.

pricing_file.verified_on: 2026-06-12 (groq VERIFIED from live pricing page; other lanes still UNVERIFIED - fill or drop before render)
pricing_file.note: EP007 pricing INPUTS. Every number must be re-checked at its URL before the recording run — published rates drift and this channel does not bake stale dollars into a render. Set verified_on when you check.

integrity_notes: tok/s, latency and (if --measure-power) wall power are MEASURED per prompt on the frozen workload.json; medians reported.
hosted lanes run the same prompts non-streamed; free-tier rate limits can inflate hosted latency — runs are paced and the pause is recorded in bench_config.

all $ figures are MODELED from inputs you can audit: electricity basis = EP002's helper on measured tok/s; API rates come from ep007_pricing.json, which carries a source URL and verified-on date next to every number.
break-even formula: tokens/month where API rent equals electricity + hardware/amortization; run `python run_breakeven.py --selftest` to verify the math against hand calculations.
the Google lane (if run) is a different model class — speed/price reference only, never the same-model fair fight.
